 init(): boolean {\\\\\\\\n    if (this.initialized) return true;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    console.log('[VoiceBrain] Initializing...');\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    const SR = (window as any).SpeechRecognition || (window as any).webkitSpeechRecognition;\\\\\\\\n    if (!SR) {\\\\\\\\n      this.updateState({ error: 'Speech not supported. Use Chrome.' });\\\\\\\\n      console.error('[VoiceBrain] Speech Recognition not supported');\\\\\\\\n      return false;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    this.synth = window.speechSynthesis;\\\\\\\\n    this.recognition = new SR();\\\\\\\\n    this.recognition.continuous = true;\\\\\\\\n    this.recognition.interimResults = true;\\\\\\\\n    this.recognition.lang = 'en-US';\\\\\\\\n    this.recognition.maxAlternatives = 1;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    this.recognition.onstart = () => {\\\\\\\\n      console.log('[VoiceBrain] \\\\\\\\u2705 LISTENING');\\\\\\\\n      this.updateState({ audioState: 'LISTENING', error: null });\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    this.recognition.onresult = (event: SpeechRecognitionEvent) => {\\\\\\\\n      const result = event.results[event.results.length - 1];\\\\\\\\n      const text = result[0]?.transcript?.trim() || '';\\\\\\\\n      const isFinal = result.isFinal;\\\\\\\\n      \\\\\\\\n      if (isFinal && text) {\\\\\\\\n        console.log('[VoiceBrain] Heard:', text);\\\\\\\\n        this.updateState({ transcript: text, interim: '' });\\\\\\\\n        this.processText(text.toLowerCase());\\\\\\\\n      } else {\\\\\\\\n        this.updateState({ interim: text });\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    this.recognition.onerror = (event: SpeechRecognitionErrorEvent) => {\\\\\\\\n      console.log('[VoiceBrain] Error:', event.error);\\\\\\\\n      if (event.error === 'not-allowed') {\\\\\\\\n        this.updateState({ \\\\\\\\n          audioState: 'IDLE', \\\\\\\\n          error: 'Microphone blocked. Click \\\\\\\\ud83d\\\\\\\\udd12 in address bar \\\\\\\\u2192 Allow' \\\\\\\\n        });\\\\\\\\n        this.shouldRestart = false;\\\\\\\\n      } else if (event.error !== 'no-speech' && event.error !== 'aborted') {\\\\\\\\n        this.updateState({ error: `Error: ${event.error}` });\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    this.recognition.onend = () => {\\\\\\\\n      console.log('[VoiceBrain] Ended, shouldRestart:', this.shouldRestart, 'state:', this.state.audioState);\\\\\\\\n      if (this.shouldRestart && this.state.audioState !== 'SPEAKING' && this.state.audioState !== 'PAUSED') {\\\\\\\\n        setTimeout(() => {\\\\\\\\n          if (this.shouldRestart && this.recognition) {\\\\\\\\n            try { \\\\\\\\n              this.recognition.start(); \\\\\\\\n            } catch (e) { \\\\\\\\n              console.log('[VoiceBrain] Restart failed:', e);\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n          }\\\\\\\\n        }, 100);\\\\\\\\n      } else if (this.state.audioState !== 'SPEAKING' && this.state.audioState !== 'PAUSED') {\\\\\\\\n        this.updateState({ audioState: 'IDLE' });\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    this.initialized = true;\\\\\\\\n    console.log('[VoiceBrain] \\\\\\\\u2705 Initialized');\\\\\\\\n    return true;\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  // \\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\n  // STATE MANAGEMENT\\\\\\\\n  // \\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\n  private updateState(partial: Partial<BrainState>): void {\\\\\\\\n    this.state = { ...this.state, ...partial };\\\\\\\\n    this.listeners.forEach(l => l(this.state));\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  getState(): BrainState {\\\\\\\\n    return { ...this.state };\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  subscribe(listener: StateListener): () => void {\\\\\\\\n    this.listeners.add(listener);\\\\\\\\n    listener(this.state);\\\\\\\\n    return () => this.listeners.delete(listener);\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  onDigits(handler: DigitHandler): () => void {\\\\\\\\n    this.digitHandlers.add(handler);\\\\\\\\n    return () => this.digitHandlers.delete(handler);\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  onCommand(handler: CommandHandler): () => void {\\\\\\\\n    this.commandHandlers.add(handler);\\\\\\\\n    return () => this.commandHandlers.delete(handler);\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  setContext(context: string): void {\\\\\\\\n    this.updateState({ context });\\\\\\\\n    console.log('[VoiceBrain] Context:', context);\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  setMode(mode: VoiceMode): void {\\\\\\\\n    this.updateState({ mode });\\\\\\\\n    console.log('[VoiceBrain] Mode:', mode);\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  // \\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\n  // TEXT PROCESSING\\\\\\\\n  // \\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\n  private processText(text: string): void {\\\\\\\\n    // Pause commands\\\\\\\\n    if (text.includes('hey') || text === 'stop' || text === 'pause' || text.includes('wait')) {\\\\\\\\n      this.pause();\\\\\\\\n      this.speak(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"I'm here when you need me. Say resume to continue.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    // Resume commands\\\\\\\\n    if (text === 'resume' || text === 'continue' || text === 'go ahead') {\\\\\\\\n      this.resume();\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    // Mode switching\\\\\\\\n    if (text.includes('talkative mode')) {\\\\\\\\n      this.setMode('talkative');\\\\\\\\n      this.speak('Talkative mode. I will give more details.');\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    if (text.includes('text mode') || text.includes('silent')) {\\\\\\\\n      this.setMode('text');\\\\\\\\n      this.speak('Text mode. I will stay quiet.');\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    if (text.includes('interactive mode')) {\\\\\\\\n      this.setMode('interactive');\\\\\\\\n      this.speak('Interactive mode.');\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    // Extract digits\\\\\\\\n    const digits = extractDigits(text);\\\\\\\\n    if (digits) {\\\\\\\\n      console.log('[VoiceBrain] Digits:', digits);\\\\\\\\n      this.digitHandlers.forEach(h => h(digits));\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    // Dispatch to command handlers\\\\\\\\n    this.commandHandlers.forEach(h => h(text));\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  // \\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\n  // AUDIO CONTROL (with Mutex)\\\\\\\\n  // \\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\n  start(): void {\\\\\\\\n    if (!this.init()) return;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    // Audio Mutex: Don't start listening if speaking\\\\\\\\n    if (this.state.audioState === 'SPEAKING') {\\\\\\\\n      console.log('[VoiceBrain] Cannot start while speaking');\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    this.shouldRestart = true;\\\\\\\\n    if (this.recognition) {\\\\\\\\n      try { \\\\\\\\n        this.recognition.start(); \\\\\\\\n      } catch (e) { \\\\\\\\n        console.log('[VoiceBrain] Start error (may already be running):', e);\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  stop(): void {\\\\\\\\n    this.shouldRestart = false;\\\\\\\\n    if (this.recognition) {\\\\\\\\n      try { this.recognition.stop(); } catch (e) {}\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    this.updateState({ audioState: 'IDLE' });\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  pause(): void {\\\\\\\\n    this.shouldRestart = false;\\\\\\\\n    if (this.recognition) {\\\\\\\\n      try { this.recognition.stop(); } catch (e) {}\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    this.updateState({ audioState: 'PAUSED' });\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  resume(): void {\\\\\\\\n    this.shouldRestart = true;\\\\\\\\n    this.updateState({ audioState: 'LISTENING' });\\\\\\\\n    if (this.recognition) {\\\\\\\\n      try { this.recognition.start(); } catch (e) {}\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    this.speak(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"I'm listening.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  // \\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\n  // TEXT-TO-SPEECH (with Mutex)\\\\\\\\n  // \\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\n  speak(text: string, onDone?: () => void): void {\\\\\\\\n    if (!text) {\\\\\\\\n      onDone?.();\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    // Text mode = silent\\\\\\\\n    if (this.state.mode === 'text') {\\\\\\\\n      console.log('[VoiceBrain] Text mode, skipping TTS:', text);\\\\\\\\n      onDone?.();\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    if (!this.init() || !this.synth) {\\\\\\\\n      onDone?.();\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    console.log('[VoiceBrain] Speaking:', text);\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    // AUDIO MUTEX: Stop listening while speaking\\\\\\\\n    const wasListening = this.shouldRestart;\\\\\\\\n    this.shouldRestart = false;\\\\\\\\n    if (this.recognition) {\\\\\\\\n      try { this.recognition.stop(); } catch (e) {}\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    // Cancel any ongoing speech\\\\\\\\n    this.synth.cancel();\\\\\\\\n    this.updateState({ audioState: 'SPEAKING' });\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    const utterance = new SpeechSynthesisUtterance(text);\\\\\\\\n    utterance.rate = 1.0;\\\\\\\\n    utterance.pitch = 1.0;\\\\\\\\n    utterance.volume = 1.0;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    // Get best voice\\\\\\\\n    const voices = this.synth.getVoices();\\\\\\\\n    const voice = voices.find(v => v.name.includes('Samantha')) ||\\\\\\\\n                  voices.find(v => v.name.includes('Google') && v.lang.startsWith('en')) ||\\\\\\\\n                  voices.find(v => v.lang.startsWith('en-US') && !v.name.includes('Compact'));\\\\\\\\n    if (voice) utterance.voice = voice;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    utterance.onend = () => {\\\\\\\\n      console.log('[VoiceBrain] Speech ended');\\\\\\\\n      this.updateState({ audioState: wasListening ? 'LISTENING' : 'IDLE' });\\\\\\\\n      onDone?.();\\\\\\\\n      \\\\\\\\n      // Resume listening if was listening before\\\\\\\\n      if (wasListening) {\\\\\\\\n        this.shouldRestart = true;\\\\\\\\n        setTimeout(() => this.start(), 200);\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    utterance.onerror = (e) => {\\\\\\\\n      console.log('[VoiceBrain] Speech error:', e);\\\\\\\\n      this.updateState({ audioState: 'IDLE' });\\\\\\\\n      onDone?.();\\\\\\\\n      \\\\\\\\n      if (wasListening) {\\\\\\\\n        this.shouldRestart = true;\\\\\\\\n        setTimeout(() => this.start(), 200);\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    // Small delay then speak\\\\\\\\n    setTimeout(() => {\\\\\\\\n      if (this.synth) {\\\\\\\\n        this.synth.speak(utterance);\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    }, 50);\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  stopSpeaking(): void {\\\\\\\\n    if (this.synth) {\\\\\\\\n      this.synth.cancel();\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    if (this.state.audioState === 'SPEAKING') {\\\\\\\\n      this.updateState({ audioState: 'IDLE' });\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n// Export singleton\\\\\\\\nexport const voiceBrain = VoiceBrainClass.getInstance();\\\\\\\\nexport default voiceBrain;\\\\\\\\nVBEOF\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\necho \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"  \\\\\\\\u2705 function useVoice() {\\\\\\\\n  const [, forceUpdate] = useState(0);\\\\\\\\n  \\\\\\\\n  useEffect(() => {\\\\\\\\n    voiceBrain.init();\\\\\\\\n    return voiceBrain.subscribe(() => forceUpdate(x => x + 1));\\\\\\\\n  }, []);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  const state = voiceBrain.getState();\\\\\\\\n  return {\\\\\\\\n    ...state,\\\\\\\\n    isListening: state.audioState === 'LISTENING',\\\\\\\\n    isSpeaking: state.audioState === 'SPEAKING',\\\\\\\\n    isPaused: state.audioState === 'PAUSED',\\\\\\\\n    isIdle: state.audioState === 'IDLE',\\\\\\\\n    start: () => voiceBrain.start(),\\\\\\\\n    stop: () => voiceBrain.stop(),\\\\\\\\n    pause: () => voiceBrain.pause(),\\\\\\\\n    resume: () => voiceBrain.resume(),\\\\\\\\n    speak: (text: string, onDone?: () => void) => voiceBrain.speak(text, onDone),\\\\\\\\n    stopSpeaking: () => voiceBrain.stopSpeaking(),\\\\\\\\n    setMode: (mode: VoiceMode) => voiceBrain.setMode(mode),\\\\\\\\n    setContext: (ctx: string) => voiceBrain.setContext(ctx)\\\\\\\\n  };\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n// \\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\n// PROPS\\\\\\\\n// \\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\u2500\\\\\\\\n// AgenticBar: React.FC<AgenticBarProps> = ({\\\\\\\\n  context,\\\\\\\\n  hints,\\\\\\\\n  welcomeMessage,\\\\\\\\n  autoStart = true,\\\\\\\\n  showTypeInput = true,\\\\\\\\n  onDigits: onDigitsProp,\\\\\\\\n  onCommand: onCommandProp\\\\\\\\n}) => {\\\\\\\\n  const voice = useVoice();\\\\\\\\n  const welcomeSpoken = useRef(false);\\\\\\\\n  const [textInput, setTextInput] = useState('');\\\\\\\\n  const [showModes, setShowModes] = useState(false);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  // Register digit handler\\\\\\\\n  useEffect(() => {\\\\\\\\n    if (onDigitsProp) {\\\\\\\\n      return voiceBrain.onDigits(onDigitsProp);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }, [onDigitsProp]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  // Register command handler\\\\\\\\n  useEffect(() => {\\\\\\\\n    if (onCommandProp) {\\\\\\\\n      return voiceBrain.onCommand(onCommandProp);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }, [onCommandProp]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  // Set context\\\\\\\\n  useEffect(() => {\\\\\\\\n    if (context) {\\\\\\\\n      voiceBrain.setContext(context);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }, [context]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  // Welcome message + auto-start\\\\\\\\n  useEffect(() => {\\\\\\\\n    if (!welcomeSpoken.current) {\\\\\\\\n      welcomeSpoken.current = true;\\\\\\\\n      if (welcomeMessage) {\\\\\\\\n        setTimeout(() => {\\\\\\\\n          voiceBrain.speak(welcomeMessage, () => {\\\\\\\\n            if (autoStart) {\\\\\\\\n              voiceBrain.start();\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n          });\\\\\\\\n        }, 600);\\\\\\\\n      } else if (autoStart) {\\\\\\\\n        setTimeout(() => voiceBrain.start(), 500);\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }, [welcomeMessage, autoStart]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  // Handle text input submit\\\\\\\\n  const handleTextSubmit = useCallback(() => {\\\\\\\\n    if (textInput.trim()) {\\\\\\\\n      const digits = extractDigits(textInput);\\\\\\\\n      if (digits && onDigitsProp) {\\\\\\\\n        onDigitsProp(digits);\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n      if (onCommandProp) {\\\\\\\\n        onCommandProp(textInput.toLowerCase());\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n      setTextInput('');\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }, [textInput, onDigitsProp, onCommandProp]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  // Colors based on state\\\\\\\\n  const stateColors: Record<AudioState, string> = {\\\\\\\\n    'IDLE': '#666',\\\\\\\\n    'LISTENING': '#4CAF50',\\\\\\\\n    'SPEAKING': '#FFD700',\\\\\\\\n    'PAUSED': '#FF9800'\\\\\\\\n  };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  const stateIcons: Record<AudioState, string> = {\\\\\\\\n    'IDLE': '\\\\\\\\u25a0',\\\\\\\\n    'LISTENING': '\\\\\\\\ud83c\\\\\\\\udfa4',\\\\\\\\n    'SPEAKING': '\\\\\\\\ud83d\\\\\\\\udd0a',\\\\\\\\n    'PAUSED': '\\\\\\\\u23f8\\\\\\\\ufe0f'\\\\\\\\n  };\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  const statusColor = stateColors[voice.audioState];\\\\\\\\n  const statusIcon = stateIcons[voice.audioState];\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  return (\\\\\\\\n    <div style={{\\\\\\\\n      margin: '16px 0 0',\\\\\\\\n      padding: '16px',\\\\\\\\n      background: `linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(${voice.isListening ? '76,175,80' : voice.isSpeaking ? '255,215,0' : '40,40,40'}, 0.15), rgba(0,0,0,0.3))`,\\\\\\\\n      border: `2px solid ${statusColor}`,\\\\\\\\n      borderRadius: '16px'\\\\\\\\n    }}>\\\\\\\\n      {/* Status Row */}\\\\\\\\n      <div style={{ \\\\\\\\n        display: 'flex', \\\\\\\\n        alignItems: 'center', \\\\\\\\n        justifyContent: 'space-between', \\\\\\\\n        marginBottom: '12px' \\\\\\\\n      }}>\\\\\\\\n        <div style={{ display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', gap: '12px' }}>\\\\\\\\n          <span style={{\\\\\\\\n            width: '16px',\\\\\\\\n            height: '16px',\\\\\\\\n            borderRadius: '50%',\\\\\\\\n            background: statusColor,\\\\\\\\n            boxShadow: (voice.isListening || voice.isSpeaking) ? `0 0 12px ${statusColor}` : 'none',\\\\\\\\n            animation: voice.isListening ? 'agenticPulse 1.5s infinite' : 'none'\\\\\\\\n          }} />\\\\\\\\n          <span style={{ color: statusColor, fontWeight: 700 }}>\\\\\\\\n            {statusIcon} {voice.audioState}\\\\\\\\n          </span>\\\\\\\\n          {context && (\\\\\\\\n            <span style={{ color: '#888', fontSize: '0.85em' }}>\\\\\\\\u2022 {context}</span>\\\\\\\\n          )}\\\\\\\\n          <span style={{\\\\\\\\n            padding: '2px 8px',\\\\\\\\n            background: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.1)',\\\\\\\\n            borderRadius: '10px',\\\\\\\\n            fontSize: '0.7em',\\\\\\\\n            color: '#aaa',\\\\\\\\n            textTransform: 'uppercase'\\\\\\\\n          }}>\\\\\\\\n            {voice.mode}\\\\\\\\n          </span>\\\\\\\\n        </div>\\\\\\\\n      </div>\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n      {/* Buttons */}\\\\\\\\n      <div style={{ display: 'flex', gap: '8px', marginBottom: '12px', flexWrap: 'wrap' }}>\\\\\\\\n        {/* Listen/Stop */}\\\\\\\\n        <button\\\\\\\\n          onClick={() => voice.isListening ? voice.stop() : voice.start()}\\\\\\\\n          style={{\\\\\\\\n            padding: '10px 16px',\\\\\\\\n            background: voice.isListening ? '#4CAF50' : 'rgba(255,255,255,0.1)',\\\\\\\\n            color: voice.isListening ? '#fff' : '#aaa',\\\\\\\\n            border: voice.isListening ? 'none' : '1px solid #555',\\\\\\\\n            borderRadius: '12px',\\\\\\\\n            cursor: 'pointer',\\\\\\\\n            fontWeight: 600\\\\\\\\n          }}\\\\\\\\n        >\\\\\\\\n          \\\\\\\\ud83c\\\\\\\\udfa4 {voice.isListening ? 'Stop' : 'Listen'}\\\\\\\\n        </button>\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        {/* Speak Test */}\\\\\\\\n        <button\\\\\\\\n          onClick={() => voice.speak('Hello! I am Mr. V, your VistaView assistant.')}\\\\\\\\n          style={{\\\\\\\\n            padding: '10px 16px',\\\\\\\\n            background: voice.isSpeaking ? '#FFD700' : 'rgba(255,255,255,0.1)',\\\\\\\\n            color: voice.isSpeaking ? '#000' : '#aaa',\\\\\\\\n            border: voice.isSpeaking ? 'none' : '1px solid #555',\\\\\\\\n            borderRadius: '12px',\\\\\\\\n            cursor: 'pointer',\\\\\\\\n            fontWeight: 600\\\\\\\\n          }}\\\\\\\\n        >\\\\\\\\n          \\\\\\\\ud83d\\\\\\\\udd0a Speak\\\\\\\\n        </button>\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        {/* Pause/Resume */}\\\\\\\\n        <button\\\\\\\\n          onClick={() => voice.isPaused ? voice.resume() : voice.pause()}\\\\\\\\n          style={{\\\\\\\\n            padding: '10px 16px',\\\\\\\\n            background: voice.isPaused ? '#FF9800' : 'rgba(255,255,255,0.1)',\\\\\\\\n            color: voice.isPaused ? '#fff' : '#aaa',\\\\\\\\n            border: voice.isPaused ? 'none' : '1px solid #555',\\\\\\\\n            borderRadius: '12px',\\\\\\\\n            cursor: 'pointer',\\\\\\\\n            fontWeight: 600\\\\\\\\n          }}\\\\\\\\n        >\\\\\\\\n          {voice.isPaused ? Here\u2019s how to make a new chat pick up from where the last one stopped without losing your whole setup.\n\ud83d\udd01 1. Always save a small context file at the end of each session\nIn your working folder (or cloud drive) keep one file:\n\n```\n.vv_context/session_state.json\n\n```\n\nEach time you stop, save:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"timestamp\": \"2026-01-04T20:45:00Z\",\n  \"project_id\": \"vistaview_main\",\n  \"session_id\": \"vv_session_20260104_2045\",\n  \"last_backup\": \"/backups/20260104_204400/\",\n  \"active_ruleset\": \"VISTAVIEW_AGENTIC_STANDARD_v1.1\",\n  \"manifest_path\": \"/Users/vistaview/vv_main_stack/.vv_context/manifest.json\",\n  \"current_task\": \"Agentic Bar mic validation\",\n  \"checkpoint_log\": \"/logs/20260104_2040_sanity.log\",\n  \"progress_notes\": \"Pointer actions stable; mic wake-word drift observed\"\n}\n\n```\n\nThis is your \u201csave point.\u201d\n\ud83e\udde9 2. In the new chat, start with a restore command\nOpen a new chat and paste:\n\n```\nRESTORE_CONTEXT: /Users/vistaview/vv_main_stack/.vv_context/manifest.json\nRESUME_SESSION_STATE: /Users/vistaview/vv_main_stack/.vv_context/session_state.json\nFOLLOW_RULES: VISTAVIEW_AGENTIC_STANDARD_v1.1\n\n```\n\nThat tells the agent:\n\u201cLoad my last environment, same project ID, ruleset, and continue from the checkpoint.\u201d\nIt can now reload your folder structure, embeddings, manifest, logs, and continue exactly where it left off.\n\u2699\ufe0f 3. Automate it (so you never type again)\nCreate a script:\n\n```bash\n#!/bin/bash\n# vv_resume.sh\nVV_HOME=\"$HOME/vv_main_stack\"\necho \"\ud83e\udde0 Restoring VistaView Agentic Session...\"\ncat \"$VV_HOME/.vv_context/session_state.json\"\necho \"-----\"\necho \"LOAD_CONTEXT:$VV_HOME/.vv_context/manifest.json\"\necho \"RESUME_SESSION_STATE:$VV_HOME/.vv_context/session_state.json\"\necho \"FOLLOW_RULES:VISTAVIEW_AGENTIC_STANDARD_v1.1\"\n\n```\n\nThen in any new chat, just paste the output of:\n\n```\nbash vv_resume.sh\n\n```\n\nand you\u2019ll have an instant bootstrapped context.\n\ud83d\udcbe 4. Optional: Persistent background \u201cstate daemon\u201d\nIf your cloud allows small background scripts, keep a lightweight watcher that:\n* Monitors `/logs/` and `/session_state.json`\n* Auto-saves every few minutes\n* Updates the checkpoint whenever the session changes task\nThat way any new chat or notebook just reads the latest timestamp automatically.\n\ud83e\udde0 5. The practical mental model\nThink of every chat as a stateless interface that must rehydrate its brain from a shared \u201cmemory file.\u201d So the golden rule:\nBefore closing \u2192 save session_state.json When opening new \u2192 load manifest + resume session_state.json\nThat\u2019s all you need. No re-feeding files manually, no forgotten variables, no starting from scratch. wantto open ne chat create requried and continue the same in new chat session of claude",
    "citations": []
  }
]

When Claude needs to work with professional documents (.docx files) for: (1) Creating new documents, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with tracked changes, (4) Adding comments, or any other document tasks\"\n     4\tlicense: Proprietary. LICENSE.txt has complete terms\n     5\t---\n     6\t\n     7\t# DOCX creation, editing, and analysis\n     8\t\n     9\t## Overview\n    10\t\n    11\tA user may ask you to create, edit, or analyze the contents of a .docx file. A .docx file is essentially a ZIP archive containing XML files and other resources that you can read or edit. You have different tools and workflows available for different tasks.\n    12\t\n    13\t## Workflow Decision Tree\n    14\t\n    15\t### Reading/Analyzing Content\n    16\tUse \"Text extraction\" or \"Raw XML access\" sections below\n    17\t\n    18\t### Creating New Document\n    19\tUse \"Creating a new Word document\" workflow\n    20\t\n    21\t### Editing Existing Document\n    22\t- **Your own document + simple changes**\n    23\t  Use \"Basic OOXML editing\" workflow\n    24\t\n    25\t- **Someone else's document**\n    26\t  Use **\"Redlining workflow\"** (recommended default)\n    27\t\n    28\t- **Legal, academic, business, or government docs**\n    29\t  Use **\"Redlining workflow\"** (required)\n    30\t\n    31\t## Reading and analyzing content\n    32\t\n    33\t### Text extraction\n    34\tIf you just need to read the text contents of a document, you should convert the document to markdown using pandoc. Pandoc provides excellent support for preserving document structure and can show tracked changes:\n    35\t\n    36\t```bash\n    37\t# Convert document to markdown with tracked changes\n    38\tpandoc --track-changes=all path-to-file.docx -o output.md\n    39\t# Options: --track-changes=accept/reject/all\n    40\t```\n    41\t\n    42\t### Raw XML access\n    43\tYou need raw XML access for: comments, complex formatting, document structure, embedded media, and metadata. For any of these features, you'll need to unpack a document and read its raw XML contents.\n    44\t\n    45\t#### Unpacking a file\n    46\t`python ooxml/scripts/unpack.py <office_file> <output_directory>`\n    47\t\n    48\t#### Key file structures\n    49\t* `word/document.xml` - Main document contents\n    50\t* `word/comments.xml` - Comments referenced in document.xml\n    51\t* `word/media/` - Embedded images and media files\n    52\t* Tracked changes use `<w:ins>` (insertions) and `<w:del>` (deletions) tags\n    53\t\n    54\t## Creating a new Word document\n    55\t\n    56\tWhen creating a new Word document from scratch, use **docx-js**, which allows you to create Word documents using JavaScript/TypeScript.\n    57\t\n    58\t### Workflow\n    59\t1. **MANDATORY - READ ENTIRE FILE**: Read [`docx-js.md`](docx-js.md) (~500 lines) completely from start to finish. **NEVER set any range limits when reading this file.** Read the full file content for detailed syntax, critical formatting rules, and best practices before proceeding with document creation.\n    60\t2. Create a JavaScript/TypeScript file using Document, Paragraph, TextRun components (You can assume all dependencies are installed, but if not, refer to the dependencies section below)\n    61\t3. Export as .docx using Packer.toBuffer()\n    62\t\n    63\t## Editing an existing Word document\n    64\t\n    65\tWhen editing an existing Word document, use the **Document library** (a Python library for OOXML manipulation). The library automatically handles infrastructure setup and provides methods for document manipulation. For complex scenarios, you can access the underlying DOM directly through the library.\n    66\t\n    67\t### Workflow\n    68\t1. **MANDATORY - READ ENTIRE FILE**: Read [`ooxml.md`](ooxml.md) (~600 lines) completely from start to finish. **NEVER set any range limits when reading this file.** Read the full file content for the Document library API and XML patterns for directly editing document files.\n    69\t2. Unpack the document: `python ooxml/scripts/unpack.py <office_file> <output_directory>`\n    70\t3. Create and run a Python script using the Document library (set PYTHONPATH per \"Initialization\" section in ooxml.md)\n    71\t4. Pack the final document: `python ooxml/scripts/pack.py <input_directory> <office_file>`\n    72\t\n    73\tThe Document library provides both high-level methods for common operations and direct DOM access for complex scenarios.\n    74\t\n    75\t## Redlining workflow for document review\n    76\t\n    77\tThis workflow allows you to plan comprehensive tracked changes using markdown before implementing them in OOXML. **CRITICAL**: For complete tracked changes, you must implement ALL changes systematically.\n    78\t\n    79\t**Batching Strategy**: Group related changes into batches of 3-10 changes. This makes debugging manageable while maintaining efficiency. Test each batch before moving to the next.\n    80\t\n    81\t**Principle: Minimal, Precise Edits**\n    82\tWhen implementing tracked changes, only mark text that actually changes. Repeating unchanged text makes edits harder to review and appears unprofessional. Break replacements into: [unchanged text] + [deletion] + [insertion] + [unchanged text]. Preserve the original run's RSID for unchanged text by extracting the `<w:r>` element from the original and reusing it.\n    83\t\n    84\tExample - Changing \"30 days\" to \"60 days\" in a sentence:\n    85\t```python\n    86\t# BAD - Replaces entire sentence\n    87\t'<w:del><w:r><w:delText>The term is 30 days.</w:delText></w:r></w:del><w:ins><w:r><w:t>The term is 60 days.</w:t></w:r></w:ins>'\n    88\t\n    89\t# GOOD - Only marks what changed, preserves original <w:r> for unchanged text\n    90\t'<w:r w:rsidR=\"00AB12CD\"><w:t>The term is </w:t></w:r><w:del><w:r><w:delText>30</w:delText></w:r></w:del><w:ins><w:r><w:t>60</w:t></w:r></w:ins><w:r w:rsidR=\"00AB12CD\"><w:t> days.</w:t></w:r>'\n    91\t```\n    92\t\n    93\t### Tracked changes workflow\n    94\t\n    95\t1. **Get markdown representation**: Convert document to markdown with tracked changes preserved:\n    96\t   ```bash\n    97\t   pandoc --track-changes=all path-to-file.docx -o current.md\n    98\t   ```\n    99\t\n   100\t2. **Identify and group changes**: Review the document and identify ALL changes needed, organizing them into logical batches:\n   101\t\n   102\t   **Location methods** (for finding changes in XML):\n   103\t   - Section/heading numbers (e.g., \"Section 3.2\", \"Article IV\")\n   104\t   - Paragraph identifiers if numbered\n   105\t   - Grep patterns with unique surrounding text\n   106\t   - Document structure (e.g., \"first paragraph\", \"signature block\")\n   107\t   - **DO NOT use markdown line numbers** - they don't map to XML structure\n   108\t\n   109\t   **Batch organization** (group 3-10 related changes per batch):\n   110\t   - By section: \"Batch 1: Section 2 amendments\", \"Batch 2: Section 5 updates\"\n   111\t   - By type: \"Batch 1: Date corrections\", \"Batch 2: Party name changes\"\n   112\t   - By complexity: Start with simple text replacements, then tackle complex structural changes\n   113\t   - Sequential: \"Batch 1: Pages 1-3\", \"Batch 2: Pages 4-6\"\n   114\t\n   115\t3. **Read documentation and unpack**:\n   116\t   - **MANDATORY - READ ENTIRE FILE**: Read [`ooxml.md`](ooxml.md) (~600 lines) completely from start to finish. **NEVER set any range limits when reading this file.** Read the full file content for the Document library API and XML patterns for directly editing document files.\n   117\t   - **Unpack the document**: `python ooxml/scripts/unpack.py <file.docx> <dir>`\n   118\t   - **Note the suggested RSID**: The unpack script will suggest an RSID to use for your tracked changes. Copy this RSID for use in step 4b.\n   119\t\n   120\t4. **Implement changes in batches**: Group changes logically (by section, by type, or by proximity) and implement them together in a single script. This approach:\n   121\t   - Makes debugging easier (smaller batch = easier to isolate errors)\n   122\t   - Allows incremental progress\n   123\t   - Maintains efficiency (batch size of 3-10 changes works well)\n   124\t\n   125\t   **Suggested batch groupings:**\n   126\t   - By document section (e.g., \"Section 3 changes\", \"Definitions\", \"Termination clause\")\n   127\t   - By change type (e.g., \"Date changes\", \"Party name updates\", \"Legal term replacements\")\n   128\t   - By proximity (e.g., \"Changes on pages 1-3\", \"Changes in first half of document\")\n   129\t\n   130\t   For each batch of related changes:\n   131\t\n   132\t   **a. Map text to XML**: Grep for text in `word/document.xml` to verify how text is split across `<w:r>` elements.\n   133\t\n   134\t   **b. Create and run script**: Set PYTHONPATH and import Document library (see \"Initialization\" in ooxml.md), then use `get_node` to find nodes, implement changes, and `doc.save()`. See **\"Document Library\"** section in ooxml.md for patterns.\n   135\t\n   136\t   **Note**: Always grep `word/document.xml` immediately before writing a script to get current line numbers and verify text content. Line numbers change after each script run.\n   137\t\n   138\t5. **Pack the document**: After all batches are complete, convert the unpacked directory back to .docx:\n   139\t   ```bash\n   140\t   python ooxml/scripts/pack.py unpacked reviewed-document.docx\n   141\t   ```\n   142\t\n   143\t6. **Final verification**: Do a comprehensive check of the complete document:\n   144\t   - Convert final document to markdown:\n   145\t     ```bash\n   146\t     pandoc --track-changes=all reviewed-document.docx -o verification.md\n   147\t     ```\n   148\t   - Verify ALL changes were applied correctly:\n   149\t     ```bash\n   150\t     grep \"original phrase\" verification.md  # Should NOT find it\n   151\t     grep \"replacement phrase\" verification.md  # Should find it\n   152\t     ```\n   153\t   - Check that no unintended changes were introduced\n   154\t\n   155\t\n   156\t## Converting Documents to Images\n   157\t\n   158\tTo visually analyze Word documents, convert them to images using a two-step process:\n   159\t\n   160\t1. **Convert DOCX to PDF**:\n   161\t   ```bash\n   162\t   soffice --headless --convert-to pdf document.docx\n   163\t   ```\n   164\t\n   165\t2. **Convert PDF pages to JPEG images**:\n   166\t   ```bash\n   167\t   pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 document.pdf page\n   168\t   ```\n   169\t   This creates files like `page-1.jpg`, `page-2.jpg`, etc.\n   170\t\n   171\tOptions:\n   172\t- `-r 150`: Sets resolution to 150 DPI (adjust for quality/size balance)\n   173\t- `-jpeg`: Output JPEG format (use `-png` for PNG if preferred)\n   174\t- `-f N`: First page to convert (e.g., `-f 2` starts from page 2)\n   175\t- `-l N`: Last page to convert (e.g., `-l 5` stops at page 5)\n   176\t- `page`: Prefix for output files\n   177\t\n   178\tExample for specific range:\n   179\t```bash\n   180\tpdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 -f 2 -l 5 document.pdf page  # Converts only pages 2-5\n   181\t```\n   182\t\n   183\t## Code Style Guidelines\n   184\t**IMPORTANT**: When generating code for DOCX operations:\n   185\t- Write concise code\n   186\t- Avoid verbose variable names and redundant operations\n   187\t- Avoid unnecessary print statements\n   188\t\n   189\t## Dependencies\n   190\t\n   191\tRequired dependencies (install if not available):\n   192\t\n   193\t- **pandoc**: `sudo apt-get install pandoc` (for text extraction)\n   194\t- **docx**: `npm install -g docx` (for creating new documents)\n   195\t- **LibreOffice**: `sudo apt-get install libreoffice` (for PDF conversion)\n   196\t- **Poppler**: `sudo apt-get install poppler-utils` (for pdftoppm to convert PDF to images)\n   197\t- **defusedxml**: `pip install defusedxml` (for secure XML parsing)",
